quality.rationale: 结合极端相对论状态方程与静力学平衡条件进行积分推导，逻辑链条完整，具备典型的天体物理流体模型背景，且题干与解答完全对应。

origin.question: Problem 14.5. Show that a highly relativistic fluid $(\mathfrak{p} = \frac{1}{3}\rho)$ in hydrostatic equilibrium in a gravitational field can never have a free surface (i.e. a surface where $\rho\rightarrow 0$ ).
origin.answer: $\rho = C (-\mathfrak{g}_{00})^{-2}$；当 $-\mathfrak{g}_{00}$ 为有限值时 $\rho$ 不可能趋于零，因此不存在自由表面。
origin.solution: Using $\mathfrak{p} = \frac{1}{3}\rho$ in the equation of hydrostatic equilibrium (Problem 14.4) $\mathfrak{p}_{,\lambda} = (\rho +\mathfrak{p})\left[\log (-\mathfrak{g}_{00})^{\frac {1}{2}}\right]_{,\lambda}$ we get$$- \log (- g_{0 0})^{\frac {1}{2}} = \frac {1}{4} \int \frac {d \rho}{\rho}.$$This integrates to $\rho = \text{constant} \times (-\mathbf{g}_{00})^{-2}$ . As long as $\mathbf{g}_{00}$ is finite, therefore, $\rho$ cannot vanish and thus there is no free surface.
